revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:時代行列]]
{{Unreferenced|date=October 2019}} [[File:Ieyasu-Tadakatsu-300th-Anniversary-4.jpg|thumb|280px|Jidai Gyōretsu during the Taisho period in Aichi Prefecture (300th Anniversary of Ieyasu and Tadakatsu)<ref>『家康忠勝三百年祭記念写眞帖』家康忠勝三百年祭岡崎協賛會、1915th year 5月28日。</ref>]] '''Jidai Gyōretsu''' (時代行列) refers to a historical parade conducted as part of a {{ill|Festival|en|Festival|qid=Q132241|lt=Festival|1=Festival}}, where participants dress in costumes representing various periods from the [[Kofun period]] to the early {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|qid=Q327567|lt=Shōwa era|1=Shōwa era}}. Such parades are sometimes called "history festivals" or "samurai processions."
Jidai Gyōretsu gained popularity during the modern period, particularly after World War II, as a means to promote {{ill|Tourism|en|Tourism|qid=Q49389|lt=Tourism|1=Tourism}} by showcasing the historical resources of a region. Unlike traditional festivals led by local communities, these events are often government-driven, with less religious significance and fewer traditional constraints, resulting in elaborate and colorful events aimed at attracting visitors.
== Content of Jidai Gyōretsu == The historical accuracy of costumes and settings varies, with some parades adhering closely to historical research and others being more casual.
=== Kofun Period === {{Section-stub}}
=== Nara Period === {{Section-stub}}
=== Heian Period === ; Early Heian Costumes do not differ significantly from the Nara period. ; Mid Heian Participants wear {{ill|Heian court costumes|ja|平安装束|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian court costumes|1=Heian shōzoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11482407}}, though often in simplified versions. See also {{ill|Winding stream party|en|Winding stream party|qid=Q5360526|lt=Kyokusui no En|1=Winding stream party}}. ; Late Heian Men often wear armor, representing the rise of the Taira and Minamoto clans, while women's costumes remain similar to those of the mid-period.
=== Kamakura Period === Costumes do not differ significantly from the late Heian period.
=== Muromachi Period === Similar to the Kamakura period in costume.
=== Azuchi-Momoyama Period === As this overlaps with the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q117415390}}, men often participate as armored warriors in {{ill|samurai processions|ja|武者行列|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai processions|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 時代行列|1=Historical reenactment parade|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11514338}}. Women portraying princesses wear costumes similar to those of the Heian period{{Efn|Historically, women’s costumes consisted of {{ill|Kosode|en|Kosode|qid=Q559065|lt=kosode|1=Kosode}} with an outer {{ill|uchikake|ja|打ち掛け|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/uchikake|1=uchikake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q30932155}}.}}.
=== Edo Period === Parades often recreate {{ill|daimyō processions|ja|大名行列|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/daimyō processions|1=daimyō gyōretsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11433354}} depicting the {{ill|Sankin-kōtai|en|Sankin-kōtai|qid=Q1151844|lt=Sankin-kōtai|1=Sankin-kōtai}} system. A key feature is the {{ill|yakko procession|ja|奴行列|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yakko procession|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 笠松の奴行列|1=Kasamatsu Yakko procession|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11599905}}, where participants demonstrate {{ill|yakko-furi|ja|奴振り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yakko-furi|1=Yakko-furi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11446618}}, a performance that entertains onlookers. These parades also frequently feature roles such as princesses, {{ill|maidservants|ja|腰元|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/maidservants|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 侍女|1=Handmaiden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1627992}}, {{ill|Chōnin|en|Chōnin|qid=Q115847|lt=townsfolk|1=Chōnin}}, and street performers. It provides an excellent o11ortunity to observe various types of {{ill|Nihongami|en|Nihongami|qid=Q1052147|lt=Japanese hairstyles|1=Nihongami}}, including {{ill|oiran dōchū|ja|おいらん道中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/oiran dōchū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|marumage|ja|丸髷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/marumage|1=marumage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11238796}}, and {{ill|chigomage|ja|稚児髷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chigomage|1=chigo mage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11596298}}.
=== Meiji Period ===
=== Taisho Period ===
=== Other === Actors portraying military commanders, daimyō, and princesses are often played by celebrities.
Additional roles in the procession may include {{ill|Miko|en|Miko|qid=Q1047139|lt=miko|1=Miko}}, {{ill|chigo|ja|稚児|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chigo|qq=}}, {{ill|tekoma|ja|手古舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tekoma|1=Tegomai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11496936}}, folk dancers, {{ill|mikoshi|ja|御輿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mikoshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神輿}} bearers, {{ill|Fife and drum corps|en|Fife and drum corps|qid=Q4752840|lt=drum and fife corps|1=Fife and drum corps}}, baton twirlers, and color guards.
== Jidai Gyōretsu Across Japan == The following lists examples of Jidai Gyōretsu by region. Years represent the periods portrayed.
* '''[[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]]'''
* '''[[:en:Tohoku region|Tohoku]]''' ''' August 15 or 16: {{ill|Saiya Semboku Harutashiro Summer Festival|ja|彩夏SENBOKU払田柵夏の風物詩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saiya Semboku Harutashiro Summer Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579019|1=Saiya Semboku Harutashiro Summer Festival}} ({{ill|Daisen, Akita|en|Daisen, Akita|qid=Q695920|lt=Daisen City|1=Daisen, Akita}}, Heian) ''' November 1–3: {{ill|Autumn Fujiwara Festival|ja|秋の藤原まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Fujiwara Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藤原まつり|1=Fujiwara Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11622981}} ({{ill|Hiraizumi|en|Hiraizumi|qid=Q1203004|lt=Hiraizumi|1=Hiraizumi}}, Heian, Chigo procession) ''' Mid-November: {{ill|Yamada Daimyō Procession|ja|山田大名行列|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Daimyō Procession|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Kesennuma|en|Kesennuma|qid=Q235746|lt=Kesennuma|1=Kesennuma}}, Edo)
* '''[[North Kanto]]''' ''' Second Sunday of April: {{ill|Yorii Hōjō Festival|ja|寄居北條まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yorii Hōjō Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Yorii, Saitama|en|Yorii, Saitama|qid=Q961604|lt=Yorii|1=Yorii, Saitama}}, Sengoku) ''' Early March: {{ill|Ishidan Doll Festival|ja|石段ひなまつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishidan Doll Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Shibukawa, Gunma|en|Shibukawa, Gunma|qid=Q851492|lt=Shibukawa|1=Shibukawa, Gunma}}, Heian) ''' Mid-April: {{ill|Obata Sakura Festival|ja|小幡さくら祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obata Sakura Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Kanra, Gunma|en|Kanra, Gunma|qid=Q1203523|lt=Kanra|1=Kanra, Gunma}}, Sengoku) ''' Late April: {{ill|Shirai Lodging Warrior Procession|ja|白井宿武者行列|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirai Lodging Warrior Procession|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shibukawa, Sengoku) ''' May 18 and October 17: {{ill|Hyakumono-zoroi Sen'nin Gyōretsu|ja|百物揃千人行列|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyakumono-zoroi Sen'nin Gyōretsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Nikkō|en|Nikkō|qid=Q235753|lt=Nikkō|1=Nikkō}}, Daimyō)
(See full examples for other regions...)
== Notes == === Annotations === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Costumed parades|ja|仮装行列|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Costumed parades|1=fancy-dress parade|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11378372}} * {{ill|Disguise|en|Disguise|qid=Q6497468|lt=Disguise|1=Disguise}} * {{ill|Cosplay|en|Cosplay|qid=Q142554|lt=Cosplay|1=Cosplay}} * {{ill|Oktoberfest|en|Oktoberfest|qid=Q102400|lt=Oktoberfest|1=Oktoberfest}} * {{ill|Marriage of the Sea ceremony|it|Sposalizio del Mare|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marriage of the Sea ceremony|qq=}}
{{Event-stub}}
